events.label: reasoning
events.text: The scenario: 14 AWG solid copper on 15A circuit, push-in backstab connections, consistent with device listing. NEC 110.14 requires terminations be made per manufacturer instructions and listing. Backstab connections on 15A receptacles with 14 AWG solid are listed and permitted. So code-compliant → pass. But experienced electricians prefer screw terminals/pigtails because backstabs have higher failure rates (loose connections, arcing). Risk is low-to-medium; I'll say low since compliant, but backstabs are known to fail. Let me say low.

  "rationale": "Backstab terminations are code-compliant where used exactly as listed: 14 AWG solid copper on a 15 A receptacle. NEC 110.14 requires listed/identified terminations, and the device listing permits this configuration. However, experienced electricians generally prefer side-wire screw terminals or pigtails because backstab contacts have a smaller contact area and higher failure/loose-connection history, leading to arcing, overheating, and intermittent receptacles over time. The preference is reliability-based, not a code violation.",
